text: For how long did the writer give up smoking?
After reading an article entitled 'Cigarette Smoking and Your Health' I lit a cigarette to calm my nerves.
zh: 读完一篇题为《吸烟与健康》的文章后,我点了一支烟来平复情绪。

text: I smoked with concentration and pleasure as I was sure that this would be my last cigarette.
zh: 我抽得又专注又惬意,因为我确信这将是我最后一支烟。

text: For a whole week I did not smoke at all and during this time my wife suffered terribly.
zh: 那之后整整一个星期我一根烟也没抽,而这期间我妻子可吃了不少苦头。

text: I had all the usual symptoms of someone giving up smoking: a bad temper and an enormous appetite.
zh: 我出现了戒烟者常有的一切症状:脾气暴躁,食欲大增。

text: My friends kept on offering me cigarettes and cigars.
zh: 朋友们不停地给我递烟递雪茄。

text: They made no effort to hide their amusement whenever I produced a packet of sweets from my pocket.
zh: 每次我从口袋里掏出一包糖果时,他们都毫不掩饰地笑我。

text: After seven days of this I went to a party.
zh: 这样过了七天后,我去参加了一个聚会。

text: Everybody around me was smoking and I felt extremely uncomfortable.
zh: 周围的人都在抽烟,我感到极其难受。

text: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, it was more than I could bear.
zh: 当我的老朋友布莱恩硬要我接一支烟时,我实在忍不住了。

text: I took one guiltily, lit it and smoked with satisfaction.
zh: 我心虚地接过一支,点上,惬意地抽了起来。

text: My wife was delighted that things had returned to normal once more.
zh: 我妻子很高兴,一切又恢复了原样。

text: Anyway, as Brian pointed out, it is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.
zh: 不管怎样,正如布莱恩所说,戒烟其实是世界上最容易的事。

text: He himself has done it lots of times!
zh: 他自己就已经戒了好多次了!
